agent_output: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Bird requires leaderboard rankings to reflect score changes within 100ms with no stale reads. MJ's architecture routes all reads through a Fastly CDN cache with a 30-second TTL. Pippen confirms this means reads can be up to 30 seconds stale — a 300x violation of Bird's 100ms freshness requirement. However, Pippen also confirms the CDN cache is a hard operational requirement to avoid overwhelming Redis at 50,000 concurrent viewers. These two requirements are mutually exclusive as currently stated: you cannot serve fresh-within-100ms data from a cache with a 30-second TTL.","agents_involved":["Bird","MJ","Pippen"],"routed_to":"Coach K","options":["Option A: Relax Bird's domain rule. Accept 30s staleness for the general audience and reserve 100ms freshness only for the players themselves (direct Redis reads for participants, CDN for spectators). This requires Bird to redefine 'consistency' with two tiers.","Option B: Eliminate the CDN for leaderboard reads and scale Redis horizontally (read replicas or cluster) to handle 50k concurrent viewers directly. MJ and Pippen must validate that Redis can absorb this load within budget and latency targets.","Option C: Reduce CDN TTL dramatically (e.g., 1-5 seconds) and use cache invalidation (Fastly Instant Purge / surrogate keys) on every score event. This is a middle ground — not 100ms fresh, but far better than 30s. Requires Bird to accept a relaxed SLA (e.g., 5s) and MJ to add a cache-purge pathway from the score-event pipeline to Fastly.","Option D: Use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events to push leaderboard diffs directly to viewers, bypassing CDN for live data entirely. CDN serves only the initial page load. Requires MJ to redesign the read path and Pippen to validate WebSocket infrastructure at 50k connections."],"recommendation":"Option A (tiered freshness) or Option C (short TTL + active purge) are the most pragmatic. Option A cleanly separates two real user segments with different needs. Option C preserves a single read path but requires Bird to accept a relaxed SLA. Coach K should convene Bird and MJ to agree on which tradeoff is acceptable before Shaq writes any code."}],"confidence":{"level":35,"high_confidence_areas":["The contradiction is real and clearly identified by all three agents","Redis Sorted Sets are the correct data structure for ranking","CDN caching is operationally necessary at 50k viewers"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which resolution option Bird and MJ will agree on","Whether Fastly Instant Purge latency is low enough for Option C","Whether Redis read replicas alone can handle 50k direct connections for Option B"],"assumptions":["All 50,000 concurrent viewers need the leaderboard endpoint, not just a subset","The 100ms requirement applies to all consumers, not just game participants","Fastly is the only CDN option under consideration"]}}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the inputs from Bird, MJ, and Pippen for contradictions.

Bird says: 100ms consistency, no stale reads
MJ says: 30s CDN cache TTL
Pippen says: 30s CDN TTL violates Bird's 100ms requirement, but CDN is operationally required

This is a clear contradiction between Bird's domain rules and MJ's architecture. Pippen explicitly called it out. I must escalate and NOT produce a handoff brief.
